propositions: The docs, lock module, and integration test disagree on the write-lock lifecycle and must be reconciled to one consistent statement.
docs/concurrency.md I5 still documents the retired bash _flock_stage_commit (FD 200) as the active write path instead of the current Python fcntl+mkdir lock.
The lock.py docstring frames the fcntl+mkdir dual acquisition as a temporary migration window that flips dual_window to False, contradicting the test that relies on the mkdir leg as permanent.

dual_window should be treated as a permanent intentional contract: fcntl fast-local plus mkdir portable second window (atomic on POSIX / no-flock hosts like macOS), with dual_window=True as the unchanged default.

A fast deterministic test must enforce that the default write lock creates both the .ticket-write.lock fcntl file and the .ticket-write.lock.d mkdir dir, while dual_window=False takes only the fcntl leg.
The test_store_concurrency.py docstring must drop migration-window wording and align to the permanent dual-window framing.
